fields.description:
# S6 — Apply the GitHub mirror lock (repo-level ruleset, deploy-key-only push)

## Why
Enforce that GitHub `main` can ONLY be advanced by the Gerrit replication deploy key. The lock
is a REPO-LEVEL ruleset (navapbc is a free org -> admin-proof ORG-level rulesets need a paid
plan; trusted-admin editability is the accepted risk, recorded in S6-pre's ADR). This story
applies the lock that S6-pre prepared (snapshot + ADR + backend/token).

## What
- REPLACE the existing `main-protection` ruleset (id 18048287, snapshotted in S6-pre) with the
  mirror lock — the PR/status-checks model is incompatible with deploy-key-only push.
- A repo-level ruleset (enforcement `active`) on `main` (+ the branches the bot pushes): rules
  `update` + `creation` + `deletion` + `non_fast_forward`; bypass list = EXACTLY the
  replication deploy key in "Always allow" mode. A SEPARATE `refs/tags/**` ruleset with the
  SAME rules + SAME bypass (else tag pushes are rejected).
- **DeployKey bypass via a committed, idempotent `gh api` step** (the UI omits DeployKey as a
  bypass actor; terraform-provider-github historically rejects `actor_type="DeployKey"`):
  `PUT /repos/navapbc/rebar/rulesets/{id}` (full-object replacement -> idempotent), actor
  `{actor_type:"DeployKey", actor_id:null, bypass_mode:"always"}`. It is wrapped in a Terraform
  `null_resource` (`local-exec` + `depends_on`); the `github_repository_ruleset` resources
  carry **`lifecycle { ignore_changes = [enforcement] }`** so the `null_resource` owns
  `enforcement` and Terraform never reverts it to `disabled` (no state drift on re-apply).
- **Self-lockout-safe ordering, ONE `terraform apply`:** (1) create both rulesets
  `enforcement = disabled`; (2) `null_resource` registers the DeployKey bypass (`depends_on`
  the rulesets); (3) a second `null_resource` flips both to `enforcement = active`
  (`depends_on` step 2). There is never a window where enforcement is `active` with an empty
  bypass list.
- Consume S5's deploy key via a `data "github_repository_deploy_key"` (or a remote-state ref to
  S5's output) — NOT a hand-copied ID; the apply fails loudly if the key is absent.
- Mirror hygiene: turn OFF Pull Requests (Feb-2026 repo setting), disable Issues + Actions; add
  a "mirror — contribute via Gerrit at rebar.solutions.navateam.com" banner to the About +
  README; do NOT archive (archiving freezes the replication bot too).
- Rollback (runnable in ~15 min): `gh api PATCH .../rulesets/{id} -f enforcement=disabled` to
  suspend the lock, restore `main-protection` from S6-pre's snapshot, re-enable PRs/Issues/Actions.

## Scope
In: ruleset creation/enforcement + DeployKey bypass + ordering + mirror hygiene + rollback.
Out: the snapshot/ADR/token-scope pre-work (S6-pre); the Gerrit-side replication (S5).
**Dependency:** apply AFTER S6-pre (snapshot + ADR + backend ready) AND S5 (the deploy key exists).

## Files
- `infra/terraform/github.tf` — provider pin, remote backend, sensitive token var, the two
  `github_repository_ruleset` resources (created `disabled`, with `ignore_changes=[enforcement]`),
  the `data.github_repository_deploy_key` lookup, the repo-feature toggles (PRs/Issues/Actions
  off, banner), and the two `null_resource`s (bypass PUT, then enforcement flip).
- `infra/github/set-bypass-actor.sh` — the idempotent `gh api PUT` (full-object `bypass_actors`
  replacement; the validated DeployKey payload).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A direct `git push` / PR-merge to `main` by a non-bypass identity is REJECTED; force-push
      and deletion of `main` are REJECTED.
- [ ] A tag push by a non-bypass identity is REJECTED by the separate `refs/tags/**` ruleset.
- [ ] The replication deploy key CAN push `main` and tags (the SOLE bypass actor, "Always
      allow", on both rulesets).
- [ ] The DeployKey bypass is set via the committed `gh api` PUT (payload
      `{actor_type:"DeployKey", actor_id:null, bypass_mode:"always"}`), is the ONLY bypass entry,
      and re-running the apply is idempotent.
- [ ] The `github_repository_ruleset` resources carry `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [enforcement] }`;
      a repeat `terraform apply` shows NO drift on `enforcement` (the `null_resource` owns it).
- [ ] Ordering holds: rulesets created `disabled` -> bypass registered -> enforcement `active`;
      `gh api` shows each ruleset `active` WITH the DeployKey bypass present (never `active` +
      empty bypass).
- [ ] S6 obtains the deploy key via a data source / remote-state ref (not a hand-copied ID);
      applying before S5's key exists fails loudly.
- [ ] PRs disabled, Issues disabled, Actions disabled; README/About carry the mirror banner;
      repo NOT archived; the existing `main-protection` ruleset is replaced.
- [ ] A rollback runnable in ~15 min is documented (PATCH `enforcement=disabled`, restore
      `main-protection` from the S6-pre snapshot, re-enable PRs/Issues/Actions).
